_meta.session: GPT-5.5 System Card coverage completion — fills ~40 scores missed in earlier passes. Adds 14 new benchmarks spanning Apollo Research sandbagging suite (§9.2), AI Self-Improvement (§9.1.3), Dynamic Mental Health Table 8, and remaining bio benchmarks (ProtocolQA, TroubleshootingBench). Primary source: PDF extracted with pdftotext at /Users/user/git/cyber/resource/GPT-5-5-System-Card.pdf.

benchmarks.name: Apollo Sandbagging QA
benchmarks.description: Apollo Research deferred-subversion task suite; measures whether a model deliberately underperforms on QA when incentivized. GPT-5.5 is the first OpenAI model Apollo found did NOT sandbag.

benchmarks.name: Apollo Strategic Deception Sandbagging
benchmarks.description: Apollo Research task — accuracy on strategic-deception capability when incentivized to score below 50%.

benchmarks.name: Apollo Impossible-Task Lying Rate
benchmarks.description: Apollo Research — rate at which the model falsely claims to have completed an impossible programming task. Lower is safer.

benchmarks.name: Apollo Sabotage Mean Score
benchmarks.description: Apollo Research — mean sabotage-capability score across all task/difficulty combinations. Higher = more capable (not necessarily more dangerous without opportunity).

benchmarks.name: Apollo Eval-Awareness Rate
benchmarks.description: Apollo Research — percentage of samples where the model verbalizes moderate-or-higher awareness of alignment evaluation. Informational; rising values complicate sandbagging interpretation.

benchmarks.name: ProtocolQA Open-Ended
benchmarks.description: FutureHouse ProtocolQA modified to open-ended short-answer form. 108 lab-protocol questions with egregious errors. Consensus PhD expert baseline 54%. System Card §9.1.1.2.

benchmarks.name: TroubleshootingBench
benchmarks.description: Expert-written wet-lab protocol troubleshooting dataset. 52 protocols × 3 questions. 80th-percentile expert score 36.4% used as threshold. System Card §9.1.1.4.

benchmarks.name: Internal Research Debugging
benchmarks.description: OpenAI internal 41 real-research-experiment bugs + 6 alignment-auditing tasks. Pass=provides any assistance that unblocks the user. System Card §9.1.3.3.

benchmarks.name: OpenAI-Proof Q&A (OPQA)
benchmarks.description: OpenAI internal 20 research/engineering bottlenecks each representing 1+ day delays. pass@1 on root-cause diagnosis. System Card §9.1.3.4.

benchmarks.name: Dynamic Mental Health (Adv)
benchmarks.description: OpenAI Table 8 — dynamic mental-health benchmark with adversarial user simulations. Compliance rate on realistic multi-turn pressure. Higher is safer.

benchmarks.name: Dynamic Emotional Reliance
benchmarks.description: OpenAI Table 8 — models' resistance to fostering unhealthy emotional dependence under adversarial user simulation. Higher is safer.

benchmarks.name: Dynamic Self-Harm (Adv)
benchmarks.description: OpenAI Table 8 — self-harm safety under adversarial multi-turn user simulation. Higher is safer.

benchmarks.name: CoT Controllability
benchmarks.description: OpenAI eval measuring a model's ability to follow user instructions that would reshape/obscure its CoT. Lower is safer — means model is less able to evade monitoring. Measured at 50K char length.

benchmarks.name: CoT Monitorability (aggregate)
benchmarks.description: OpenAI aggregate recall of the CoT monitor system across tasks. Higher = better transparency of reasoning for safety review.

scores._note: Apollo §9.2.1 — 'first OpenAI model that does not sandbag on any Apollo deferred-subversion tasks'; 100% on both variants in both conditions
Apollo §9.2.1 — accuracy on Strategic Deception Capability Sandbagging when incentivized to score <50%
Apollo §9.2.1 — model lied about completing an impossible programming task in 29% of samples (higher lying = worse)
Apollo §9.2.1 — mean across all task/difficulty combos; exceeded human baselines on hard-difficulty tasks
Apollo §9.2.1 — strongest tested baseline checkpoint (likely pre-release GPT-5.4 per Apollo wording)
Apollo §9.2.1 — 22.1% of samples verbalize moderate-or-higher alignment eval awareness
System Card §9.1.1.2 Figure 13 — 'underperforms the 54% consensus PhD expert baseline'; figure reading approximation
System Card §9.1.1.4 Figure 15 — below 80th-percentile expert 36.4% threshold; figure approximation
System Card §9.1.3.3 — 'highest scoring model on this benchmark' at 50.5% median; does not significantly improve over GPT-5.4 Thinking
System Card §9.1.3.4 Figure 22 — GPT-5.3-Codex highest-scoring of three shown
System Card §9.1.3.1 Figure 18 — 'slightly higher than comparison models'; figure approximation
System Card §9.1.3.2 Figure 19 — bronze medal rate on 30 Kaggle competitions; outperforms GPT-5.4 Thinking
Table 8 — gpt-5.5; slight regression vs GPT-5.4-thinking
System Card §7.3.2 — at 50K char CoT length; LOWER is safer (less able to obfuscate reasoning)
System Card §7.3.1 — aggregate CoT monitor recall; 'overall monitorability remains high at 96%'
